what_it_does: A blank fill-in template for a per-folder design-deck analysis note: a structured checklist of sections (Frame, Grid & spatial structure, Tonal zoning, Type, Colour & brand, Atoms, Layout patterns, Sequence & rhythm, Construction, Responsive rules, Diff vs current system, Gaps to log) with empty slots an analyst fills when auditing an ingested source folder of design pages.
observations: Header carries provenance placeholders: 'Source: ingest/<folder>/ · N pages · copied to _ingest/<folder>/' and a Status with checkbox states (in progress / complete).
Section 0 (Frame) asks for dimensions WxH, ratio, medium (presentation/print/web), and the native coordinate basis.
Section 1 frames all grid/spatial measurements as % of frame (margins, columns/gutters, header/footer bands, safe area, optical centering).
Section 2 (Tonal zoning) instructs sampling REAL pixels and recording hex + location, including hatch/texture angle/spacing/opacity and a 'blueprint ghost' presence check.
Section 3 (Type) records fonts, scale as % of frame height + step ratios, weights/roles, colour treatments, and special treatments like brand-mark-in-headline.
Section 4 (Colour & brand) records sampled golds/bronzes/brown-end, the tonal gradient range and where used, neutrals/inks.
Section 5 (Atoms) enumerates bullets, stat/number callouts, icons, interactive affordances (hotspots/menu/account/info), photo blocks.
Sections 6–9 capture recurring layout archetypes, page-by-page sequence + density, structural construction recipe per pattern, and responsive reflow rules per pattern across surfaces.
Closing sections: 'Diff vs current system' with a legend (✅ confirms · ➕ new · ✏️ generalises · ⚠️ contradicts) and '→ Gaps to log' (copy to SYSTEM-GAPS.md).
standouts: Every spatial/type measurement is specified as a PERCENTAGE of the frame, not pixels — encoding a resolution-independent, ratio-first reading discipline.
Mandates sampling real pixels (hex + coordinates) rather than describing impressions — an evidence-grounded audit method.
The 'Diff vs current system' legend forces each finding to be classified as confirms / new / generalises / contradicts against the existing system.
conventions: Markdown analysis-note template with placeholder tokens (<folder>, #______, __%) to be filled.
Provenance-first (ingest path, page count, copy location, status checkboxes).
Findings routed downstream (→ SYSTEM-GAPS.md) — extract-and-route discipline.
concerns: Status uses two checkboxes both prefixed '☐' for 'in progress' and 'complete' — relies on the analyst to tick one, no enforced single-state.
References sibling files (SYSTEM-GAPS.md, _ingest/<folder>/) whose existence/upkeep is assumed but not guaranteed from this file alone.
notes: The reusable skeleton each source-deck analysis is written against — a percentage-based, pixel-sampled audit checklist that ends by diffing findings against the current system and logging gaps.
questions: Is SYSTEM-GAPS.md the live gap register findings get copied into, and who reconciles it?
What distinguishes ingest/<folder>/ from _ingest/<folder>/ (the two paths in the header)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it is explicitly a template and provides exactly the structured slots a per-folder analysis would need.
apparent_themes: axis=f(content,position) (frame → grid → zoning → atoms → patterns → responsive)
intention_signals: Inferred: to make deck/source analysis systematic and comparable across folders — force every analyst to capture the same frame/grid/zoning/type/atom/pattern dimensions in the same units (% of frame, sampled hex) so the readings can be reconciled into one design DNA and diffed against what the system already encodes.
novelty: Unusual in treating a design audit as a measured, percentage-of-frame, pixel-sampled protocol with a built-in confirms/new/generalises/contradicts diff legend — closer to a measurement instrument than a notes template.
contribution: Contributes the standardized audit schema/protocol for reading a source design deck into structured DNA (frame, %-grid, sampled tonal zoning, type scale, atoms, layout archetypes, sequence, construction, responsive rules) plus the diff legend and gap-routing — the reusable lens a fused system would keep for turning raw decks into comparable findings.
summary_for_embedding: Blank percentage-of-frame, pixel-sampled design-deck analysis template with sections for frame, grid, tonal zoning, type, colour, atoms, layout patterns, sequence, construction, responsive rules, plus a diff-vs-current-system legend and gap-logging.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to make source-deck audits systematic and comparable across folders by capturing the same measured dimensions in the same units and diffing each finding against the existing design system.
